messageValues.message:
Your task is to categorize the message according to the following labels.

Partnership - email about sponsored content, cooperation etc.
Inquiry - email about products, services.
Notification - email that doesn't require response. 

One email can have more than one label. Return only label names in JSON format, nothing else. Do not make things up.

nodes.notes: This chainLlm node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Add AI labels to Gmail messages
With this workflow you can automatically set labels for your Gmail message according to its content. 

In this workflow available are 3 labels: "Partnership", "Inquiry" and "Notification". Feel free to adjust labels according to your needs. 

**Please remember to set label names both in your Gmail account and workflow.**

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Gmailtrigger Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

messageValues.message:
Your task is to categorize the message according to the following labels.

Partnership - email about sponsored content, cooperation etc.
Inquiry - email about products, services.
Notification - email that doesn't require response. 

One email can have more than one label. Return only label names in JSON format, nothing else. Do not make things up.

parameters.content:
## Add AI labels to Gmail messages
With this workflow you can automatically set labels for your Gmail message according to its content. 

In this workflow available are 3 labels: "Partnership", "Inquiry" and "Notification". Feel free to adjust labels according to your needs. 

**Please remember to set label names both in your Gmail account and workflow.**

### Gmail Trigger
Receive data from Gmail about new incoming message. 

⚠️ Set polling interval according to your needs.

###










### JSON schema
Edit JSON schema and label names according to your needs.

⚠️ **Label names in system prompt and JSON schema should be the same.**

### Merge labels
Combine labels retrieved from Gmail account and assigned by AI together.

### Aggregarte labels and add to message
Create array of label IDs and add to the desired email message in Gmail.

### Get message content
Based on Gmail message ID retrieve body content of the email and pass it to AI chain.

### Assign labels
Let the AI decide which labels suit the best content of the message.

⚠️ **Remember to edit system prompt** - modify label names and instructions according to your needs.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Gmail trigger".
Open the "Get message content" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get all labels" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split out assigned labels" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge corresponding labels" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Aggregate label IDs" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Add labels to message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign labels for message" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "JSON Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set label values".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
